Raised 01-07-2025 to 30-11-2025.description: Midland Heart repairs jobs data for July to November 2025. Contains works orders, job tickets, operative assignments, completion statuses, and visit tracking information for repair jobs carried out by operatives.

July 2025.description: Midland Heart telematics data for July 2025. Contains vehicle trip logs showing operative movements, travel times, distances, and locations for repair job visits.

November 2025.description: Midland Heart telematics data for November 2025. Contains vehicle trip logs showing operative movements, travel times, distances, and locations for repair job visits.

global_rules: Telematics data: Vehicle field contains registration + operative full name; use to match with OperativeWhoCompletedJob in repairs data.
Location matching: FullAddress (repairs) can be matched with StartLocation / EndLocation (telematics) for visit verification.

file_contexts.file_path: intranet/repairs/MDH Repairs Data July - Nov 25 - DL V1.xlsx

table_rules: Jobs completed: distinct jobs where WorksOrderStatusDescription in {Complete, Closed}.
ArrivedOnSite/CompletedVisit: tablet timestamps; some operatives click through quickly (close times).
Deduplication: prefer JobTicketReference for removing duplicates; WorksOrderRef duplicates may be valid follow-ons.
Planned window: JobTicketLinePlannedStartDate/EndDate set by scheduling team.
table_contexts.table_description: Midland Heart repairs jobs data for July to November 2025. Contains works orders, job tickets, operative assignments, completion statuses, and visit tracking information for repair jobs carried out by operatives.

column_descriptions.JobTicketReference: Unique identifier for an individual job ticket. Use this field for deduplication when matching job tickets across different data sources or time periods.
column_descriptions.WorksOrderRaisedDate: Timestamp when the works order was initially raised/created in the system. Format may vary but typically includes date and time.
column_descriptions.WorksOrderIssuedDate: Timestamp when the works order was issued/dispatched to an operative for completion. This indicates when the job was assigned.
column_descriptions.WorksOrderDescription: Free-text description of the requested repair works. May include details about the issue, required repairs, tenant information, and tenant phone numbers. This is a key field for understanding what work was requested.
column_descriptions.WorksOrderStatusDescription: Lifecycle state of the works order. Common values include 'Complete' (operative finished the works) and 'Closed' (financials completed). Complete indicates technical completion; Closed indicates full administrative closure.
column_descriptions.FullAddress: Complete property address associated with the works order. Includes street address, area, city, and postcode. Use this to match with telematics data location fields.
column_descriptions.WorksOrderTargetDate: Target date and time assigned for completing the works order. This is the deadline or scheduled completion date.
column_descriptions.ScheduledAppointmentStart: Start time of the scheduled appointment window when the operative was expected to arrive. Format typically includes date and time.
column_descriptions.ScheduledAppointmentEnd: End time of the scheduled appointment window. The operative was expected to complete the visit within this time range.
column_descriptions.WorksOrderReportedCompletedDate: The date and time when all job tickets under the works order have been reported as completed. This is the actual completion timestamp.
column_descriptions.OperativeWhoCompletedJob: Full name of the operative who completed the job. Use this field along with Vehicle column from telematics data to match operatives across datasets.
column_descriptions.ArrivedOnSite: Timestamp when the operative marked arrival on site using their tablet. This is a precise GPS/time-stamped event indicating when the operative reached the property.
column_descriptions.CompletedVisit: Timestamp when the operative marked visit completion using their tablet. Times may be very close to ArrivedOnSite for quick jobs, or significantly later for longer repairs.
column_descriptions.NoAccess: Reason why access to the property was not possible. If None or empty, access was granted. Common values may include 'No answer', 'Tenant not available', etc.
column_descriptions.FollowOn: Boolean flag or indicator showing whether a follow-on visit is required. True/Yes indicates additional work or visits are needed to complete the job.
column_descriptions.FollowOnDescription: Free-text reason explaining why a follow-on appointment is required. Provides context for why the job could not be completed in a single visit.
column_descriptions.VisitDate: Timestamp when the operative accepts the job ticket on their tablet. This marks when the operative acknowledges and begins work on the job.
column_descriptions.FirstTimeFix: Indicator (boolean or flag) showing if the job was completed successfully on the first visit. True indicates successful first-time completion.
column_descriptions.SecondTimeFix: Indicator showing if the job required a second visit to complete. True indicates the job was not completed on the first attempt.
column_descriptions.ThirdTimeFix: Indicator showing if the job required a third visit to complete. True indicates multiple attempts were needed.
column_descriptions.FollowOnNotes: Free-text notes explaining why a follow-on visit is required. Provides context for why the job could not be completed in a single visit.
column_descriptions.RepairsRegion: Region where the repair job is located. Divided into geographical regions e.g. North, South, Central North, Central South, etc.
column_descriptions.RepairsPatch: Sub-region where the repair job is located in a region. Divided into geographical patches e.g. South Patch, North Patch 4, etc.

file_contexts.file_path: intranet/repairs/MDH Telematics Data July - Nov 25 - DL V1.xlsx
file_rules: Telematics tables are split by month (July, August, September, October, November 2025).

table_contexts.table_description: Midland Heart telematics data for July 2025. Contains vehicle trip logs showing operative movements, travel times, distances, and locations for repair job visits.

column_descriptions.Trip: Unique trip identifier for a vehicle journey segment.
column_descriptions.Driver: Name of the driver/operative (may be abbreviated).
column_descriptions.Vehicle: Vehicle registration number followed by operative full name. This is the best identifier for the operative who serviced the work/repair order.
column_descriptions.Departure: Date and time when the vehicle departed from the start location (format: D/M/YYYY HH:MM).
column_descriptions.StartLocation: Full street address where the trip started, including street name, area, city, and postcode.
column_descriptions.EndLocation: Full street address where the trip ended, including street name, area, city, and postcode. May also indicate 'Stopped with Ignition ON' events at specific locations.
column_descriptions.Arrival: Date and time when the vehicle arrived at the end location (format: D/M/YYYY HH:MM).
column_descriptions.Subtrip travel time: Duration of this specific trip segment in HH:MM format.
column_descriptions.Trip travel time: Total duration for the complete trip in HH:MM format. May be empty for non-travel segments (e.g., stop events).
column_descriptions.Idling time: Time spent with vehicle idling (engine running but stationary) in HH:MM format. May be empty for some segments.
column_descriptions.Business distance: Distance traveled for business purposes (repair job visits) in miles. May be empty for non-travel segments.
column_descriptions.Private distance: Distance traveled for private/non-business purposes in miles. Typically 0 for work-related trips.
column_descriptions.Total distance: Total distance traveled during the trip segment in miles. May be empty for non-travel segments.
column_descriptions.Avg speed: Average speed during the trip segment in mph. May be empty for non-travel segments or very short trips.
column_descriptions.Max speed: Maximum speed reached during the trip segment in mph. May be empty for non-travel segments.
